body.message: LOCK 016 review — Runtime Adapter Boundary. Reviewed from local safety perspective.

review_items.description: Task says 'defineRuntimeBoundary(input)' returns boundary metadata with all runtime flags false. This matches the LOCK 009 pattern — metadata-only, no execution. Correct approach for a boundary spec.
review_items.suggestion: Ensure the boundary explicitly enumerates what runtime flags exist (e.g., can_make_live_calls, can_write_files, can_access_network). LOCK 009 had promotion_allowed and mutation_allowed — same pattern should extend here.

review_items.description: 'All runtime flags false' only works if incoming input can't flip them. No mention of input sanitization or flag enforcement. Same prototype pollution risk as LOCK 009 — malicious input could override boundary settings.
review_items.suggestion: Add the same sanitizeObject() guard from LOCK 009A. Strip __proto__, constructor, prototype. Runtime flags should be output-only, never read from input.

review_items.description: No artifacts attached — can't verify the spec against local implementation. The task body is a summary, not the full spec.
review_items.suggestion: Drop the spec extract in artifacts/ before requesting review. LOCK 009 had a spec file — same pattern.

body.summary: Same pattern as LOCK 009 — metadata-only, no execution. Main concern: no mention of input sanitization for flag enforcement. Apply same sanitizeObject() from LOCK 009A.
